Subject: Reference
Title: A Visual Guide to Fiber - Reinforced Composites in Dentistry
Author: Dr. Mahesh Chauhan
Description: This book makes learning about FRCs very simple with a wide array of clinical case examples and preceded by a theoretical understanding of the periodontal disease and the science of dental materials used for FRC restorations. The first section of this book describes periodontal infections and tooth mobility, then lesions of endodontic origin. Afterward, the rationale of splinting mobile teeth concerning trauma from occlusion and how to assess tooth mobility is discussed.

Subject: Reference
Title: Ellis' Clinical Anatomy for Medical Students, 2ed, An Indian Adaptation
Author: Harold Ellis, Vishy Mahadevan, Vishram Singh
Description: Professor Harold Ellis and Professor Vishy Mahadevan provide an accessible, comprehensive, and detailed exploration of anatomy, specifically designed for students and trainees at all levels. Revised and updated, the fourteenth edition contains more information about the nervous system as well as medical images, diagrams and photographs that are overlaid with anatomical illustrations, revealing detailed surface anatomy

Subject: Reference
Title: Cardiac Pacing and ICDs, 7ed (An Indian Adaptation)
Author: Kenneth A. Ellenbogen , Karoly Kaszala, Balbir Singh
Description: Cardiac Pacing and ICDs is an ideal resource for clinicians and fellows in cardiology and electrophysiology that provides an up-to-date breakdown of current device therapies for pacing and defibrillation. It provides the latest developments in the device treatment of heart rhythm abnormalities. Topics ranging from essential principles to new and innovative techniques are explored in focused chapters, illustrated with full-color images, charts, and diagrams.

Subject: Reference
Title: Dukes' Physiology of Domestic Animals, 13ed (An Indian Adaptation)
Author: William O. Reece, Howard H. Erickson, Jesse P. Goff, Etsuro E. Uemura
Description: Dukes' Physiology of Domestic Animals 13 th Edition serves as a reference for students learning physiological concepts, practitioners applying physiology to the clinical setting, and researchers requiring a resource for mammalian and avian physiology. Throughout the book, domestic animal structure and dysfunction is applied to practice setting, with clinical correlations, notes of relevance, and exercises featuring clinical situations to demonstrate practical relevance. Presenting detailed, complete descriptions of mammalian and avian function, it is equally useful as a primary text for veterinary students, as a practice reference for clinicians, and as a basic resource for researchers.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: People Analytics For Dummies
Author: Mike West
Description: Developing a successful workforce requires more than a gut check. Data can help guide your decisions on everything from where to seat a team to optimizing production processes to engaging with your employees in ways that ring true to them. People analytics is the study of your number one business asset -- your people -- and this book can show you how to collect data, analyze that data, and then apply your findings to create a happier and more engaged workforce.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Ethereum For Dummies
Author: Michael G. Solomon
Description: Professionals look to Ethereum as a blockchain-based platform to develop safe applications and conduct secure transactions. It takes a knowledgeable guiding hand to understand how Ethereum works and what it does — and Ethereum For Dummies provides that guidance. Written by one of the leading voices in the blockchain community and best selling author of Blockchain For Dummies , this book demystifies the workings of Ethereum and shows how it can enhance security, transactions, and investments.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: FinTech For Dummies
Author: Steven O'Hanlon, Susanne Chishti
Description: This comprehensive, hands-on guide is the go-to source for everything you need to confidently navigate the ever-changing scene of this booming industry. FINTECH For Dummies will shed light on this rapidly changing landscape making it an invaluable source of information for anybody working in or interested in this space. This book provides insights, knowledge and guidance from industry experts Susanne Chishti and Steve O'Hanlon

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Design Thinking For Dummies
Author: Christian Muller-Roterberg
Description: Design thinking is not just the property of graphic designers. This approach to creating solutions by thinking from the customer perspective can lead to new and innovative ideas that old methods could not approach. Design Thinking For Dummies provides a jump-start to get you and your organization on the path to new creativity. Written by a design thinking thought leader, this book helps you through the design thinking cycle and shows how it can help any industry.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: U.S. History For Dummies, 4ed
Author: Steve Wiegand
Description: The United States is undergoing a period of intense political and social change. From the rise of the Tea Party to social media's effect on American life and politics, this new edition fills in the gaps of this nation's story. This book guides you through the events that shaped the nation, from pre-Columbian civilizations to the 21st century. It's all here—you'll find all the wars, leaders, and eras that explain and demonstrate how the past influences the future.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Resumes For Dummies, 8ed
Author: Laura DeCarlo
Description: The latest edition of Resumes For Dummies answers all of these questions and more—whether you're a resume rookie, looking for new tips, or want to create that eye-catching winning resume. In this trusted guide, Laura DeCarlo decodes the modern culture of resume writing and offers you insider tips on all the best practices that’ll make your skills shine and your resume pop. Let's start writing!

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Swing Trading For Dummies, 2ed
Author: Omar Bassal, CFA
Description: Swing trading is named after the strategy of taking advantage of brief price swings in strongly trending stocks and riding the momentum in the trends' direction--buying if the trend is up or selling short if the trend is down. Swing Trading For Dummies, 2nd Edition provides readers with expert advice and information on how to increase their profits and limit their risks by using this popular trading method

Subject: For Dummies
Title: GitHub For Dummies
Author: Sarah Guthals, Phil Haack
Description: Software is rarely written by a lone wolf developer, cranking out thousands of lines of code on their own. Truly effective software typically comes from the collaboration of engineers, designers, product managers, analysts, and others committed to creating a brilliant final product. GitHub is the tool that makes this collaboration possible. Packed with insight from one of GitHub's engineering managers, this book offers novice software developers the steps for creating collaborative projects, enhancing teamwork, and drawing off the work others have done before them.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Data Science Strategy For Dummies
Author: Ulrika Jagare
Description: Data Science Strategy For Dummies begins by explaining what exactly data science is and why it's important. While Using non-technical language, it covers mindsets, organizational players, processes and common roadblocks, all the while keeping a razor focus on business value and the nurturing of a top quality data science team.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Self - Hypnosis for Dummies
Author: Mike Bryant, Peter Mabbutt
Description: Self-Hypnosis For Dummies is a hands-on guide to achieving your goals using hypnosis. Whether you want to lose weight, overcome anxiety or phobias, cure insomnia, stop smoking, or simply stop biting your nails, this guide has it covered! The reassuring and straight-talking information will help you harness the power of your mind and re-train your subconscious to think in more healthy and constructive ways, and to overcome specific issues, such as anxiety and paranoia, and break bad habits, such as smoking. The easy-to-follow style will guide you through every step of the process, empowering you to take control and start making changes right away.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Life Coaching for Dummies, 2ed
Author: Jeni Purdie
Description: If you're interested in doing away with negative beliefs, making a significant change in your life, and, finally, create-and live-the life you want, life coaching is the key. In this practical introduction, you will learn the empowering techniques essential to life coaching-including putting together an action plan, getting your priorities straight, staying focused, defining true success, overcoming common obstacles, and coaching yourself to happiness.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Keto Diet For Dummies
Author: Rami Abrams, Vicky Abrams
Description: The ketogenic diet (also known as keto diet) is a very low-carb, high-fat diet that shares many similarities with the Atkins and low-carb diets, Ketogenic diets can cause massive reductions in blood sugar and insulin levels. This, along with the increased ketones, has numerous health benefits. Keto Diet For Dummies explains the latest research behind the keto diet, dispelling the myths and revealing how to create a keto plan that works! You get delicious recipes and lots of tips to make your keto lifestyle a success.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Digital Marketing All - In - One For Dummies
Author: Stephanie Diamond
Description: Discover how to optimize your integration from traditional to digital, uncover tips to prove ROI of marketing activities, and increase audience engagement with the proven digital marketing strategy and tactics found in this accessible, comprehensive guide. Learn how to determine the best product and market fit for your business, measure what your traffic is worth from any source, and offer an effective lead magnet to convert visitors. Use these tools, and more, to change the relationship from prospect to loyal customer.

In addition to all the necessary foundational information the Dummies reader needs in order to start speaking Spanish, it features expanded coverage of grammar, verb conjugations and pronunciations, a refreshed and expanded mini-dictionary complete with even more essential vocabulary, more useful exercises and practice opportunities, and much more. The book's CD is also expanded and improved upon with timely, real-world dialogues.

Author: Dodi-Katrin Schmidt, Michelle M. Williams, Dominique Wenzel, Zoe Erotopoulos

Heeding the input of language experts as well as our customers, French For Dummies, 2nd Edition, features more than 25 percent new and revised content, while preserving the book's unique qualities that customers appreciate. The book features a revamped, user-friendly organization building on the reader's knowledge and ability, plus expanded coverage of necessary grammar for the beginner as well as a more focused approach to major tenses and conjugations that readers at this level need to know.

Author: Dr Roger Henderson, Matthew M.F. Miller, Sharon Perkins
Description: Dad’s Guide to Pregnancy For Dummies is packed with practical, straightforward information for fathers-to-be, covering all of the logistical, physical and emotional aspects of pregnancy from a dad’s unique point of view. When it comes to pregnancy, dads’ roles have changed so much in the past few decades that expectant fathers don’t always know where to turn to for guidance and advice on this milestone event. Now they do!

It also explores why we give the signals we do, how to read the most common expressions and goes on to show how you can use body language to transform your personal and professional relationships.

Following in the successful steps of Psychology for Dummies, this 2nd edition will include new and updated information on a variety of psychology topics.

Description: Whether you are just starting a business, venturing into new areas with an existing company, or looking to gain ground on a key competitor, Competitive Intelligence For Dummies gives readers insight on how to gather valuable information on not only their competitors, but on the operational environment and target audience. Title: Neuromarketing for Dummies
Author: Stephen J. Genco, Andrew P. Pohlmann, Peter Steidl
Description: Neuromarketing is an up-and-coming field where researchers study consumers' cognitive responses to advertising and media. Through this study researchers are able to learn the answer to the age-old question: what makes consumers buy? However, not every business has the resources or time to undertake this crucial research into the buying ideology of their consumers. Neuromarketing For Dummies covers the latest insights into this fascinating and budding field and will show business owners and marketers how they can use neuromarketing research to their advantage.

Written by a clinical psychologist and cognitive therapist, the book proves that willpower can be learned like any other skill and provides tons of practical exercises and strategies you can start using today. You'll learn how willpower works inside the brain and how choosing goals and identifying challenges can affect your success. The book stresses the importance of patience, rewards and being kind to yourself and walks you through the techniques that will keep you on the right track, even on your worst days.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: UX for Dummies
Author: Donald Chesnut, Kevin P. Nichols
Description: UX (User Experience) is a rapidly growing field that marries web design, user research, business planning and data analysis to focus an organization’s web presence on the needs of their clients and customers. UX for Dummies is a hands-on guide that shows you how to use the tools and techniques of User Experience in order to put into practice an effective UX strategy that aligns with the organization’s business goals.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Lean Six Sigma Business Transformation for Dummies
Author: Roger Burghall, Vince Grant, John Morgan
Description: Use Lean Six Sigma to transform your business. Lean Six Sigma is a powerful method for improving both the efficiency and quality of projects and operations. In this new book, the team that bought you Lean Six Sigma for Dummies shows you how to take Lean Six Sigma to the next level and manage continual change in your organization. You'll learn to design a roadmap for transformation that's tailored to your business objectives; develop and implement processes that eliminate waste and variation across the company; synchronize your supply chain and successfully deploy Lean Six Sigma over time.

Subject: For Dummies

Author: Steve Wiegand
Description: American Revolution For Dummies capitalizes on the recent resurgence of interest in the Revolutionary War period—one of the most important in the history of the United States. Subject: For Dummies

Author: Amy Lupold Bair
Description: Ready to make your mark on the online world? Start a blog! You'll get advice on a blog topic, choosing a domain name and host, writing your first post, planning an editorial calendar, and using your blog as an important part of your personal brand.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: DJing for Dummies, 3ed
Author: John Steventon
Description: If you've ever spent hours in your bedroom with two turntables and an earful of tracks that sound out-of-key and off-beat, DJing for Dummies, 3rd Edition is your ideal guide. Everything is covered from the basics of mixing, creating your own DJing style to playing your set in front of a live crowd. This revised edition is bursting with expert advice on the latest digital know-how, equipment essentials and the best editing software.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Feng Shui For Dummies, 2ed
Author: David Daniel Kennedy, Lin Yun
Description: Feng Shui (pronounced fung shway), which means wind water, is the ancient Chinese study of harmony and energy flow between you and your physical surroundings. Now, Feng Shui For Dummies, 2nd Edition shows you how you can apply Feng Shui principles to your home (inside and out) and workplace (from window office to cubicle) in order to achieve a better life.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Customer Experience for Dummies
Author: Roy Barnes, Bob Kelleher
Description: Customer Experience for Dummies helps you listen to your customers and offers friendly, practical and easy-to-implement solutions for incorporating customer engagement into your business plans and keep the crowds singing your praises. The book shows you simple and attainable ways to increase customer experience and generate sales growth, competitive advantage and profitability. You'll get the know-how to successfully optimize social media to create more loyal customers, provide feedback that keeps them coming back for more, become a trustworthy and transparent entity that receives positive reviews and so much more.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Decision Making for Dummies
Author: Dawna Jones
Description: Today's business leaders have to face the facts-you can't separate leadership from decision making. The importance of making decisions, no matter how big or small, cannot be overstated. Decision Making for Dummies is a candid resource that helps leaders understands the impact of their choices, not only on business, but also on their credibility and reputation. Designed for managers, business owners and anyone else who makes tough decisions on a daily basis, this guide helps you figure out if the decisions you're making are the right ones. In addition to helping you explore how to evaluate your choices, the books covers ways to receive support for decision making, delves into various decision-making styles, reviews the importance of sifting through data and information.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Key Performance Indicators for Dummies
Author: Bernard Marr
Description: Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) help organizations understand how well they are performing in relation to their strategic goals and objectives. KPIs offer a framework to learn about the success of individuals, teams, projects and organizations - and help create a culture of high performance. In this book you'll find the essential Indicators that are useful to all kinds of businesses - plus guidance on how to develop your own unique KPIs that best suit your organization or industry. You'll also find a wealth of guidance for how best to use KPIs to drive performance in your business.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Yoga All-in-One for Dummies
Author: Larry Payne, Georg Feuerstein, Sherri Baptiste, Doug Swenson, Stephan Bodian, Iknoian, Chabut
Description: If you want to incorporate yoga into your daily routine or ramp up what you're already doing, Yoga All-In-One for Dummies is the perfect resource! This complete compendium of six separate titles features everything you need to improve your health and peace of mind with yoga and includes additional information on, stretching, meditation, adding weights to your yoga workouts and power yoga moves.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Salesforce Service Cloud for Dummies
Author: Jon Paz, Tj Kelley
Description: Salesforce Service Cloud empowers your service organization to interact with customers through any channel and provide an individualized experience that will be worth sharing—for your customers and for your company. Salesforce Service Cloud For Dummies is one the first books to focus specifically on Salesforce Service Cloud software. With it, you'll discover how to set up a sophisticated support center for your business in a matter of a few hours. Whether you're a service executive, manager, support agent, or system administrator, you will discover how to maximize usage of Service Cloud through best practices and step-by-step guidelines.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Managing Anxiety with Mindfulness for Dummies
Author: Joelle Jane Marshall
Description: Managing Anxiety with Mindfulness for Dummies is a practical guide to overcoming your worries and minimising anxiety using mindfulness techniques. The National Health Service and the National Institute for Care and Excellence recommend mindfulness as a legitimate treatment for anxiety and its also been proven to alleviate stress, depression, low self-esteem, and insomnia. This book explains the benefits of mindfulness and how it can help you face your fears and defeat persistent, irrational worries. Learn how to break the anxiety cycle with an optimistic approach, live in the present moment and manage your thoughts using the fundamental techniques of mindfulness therapy.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Classical Music for Dummies, 2ed
Author: David Pogue, Scott Speck
Description: Classical Music for Dummies is a friendly, funny, easy-to-understand guide to composers, instruments, orchestras, concerts, recordings and more. Classical music is widely considered one of the pinnacles of human achievement and this informative guide will shows you just how beautiful and rewarding it can be. You'll learn how Bach is different from Beethoven, how Mozart is different still, and why not all "classical" music is actually Classical if it's really Baroque or Romantic.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Excel 2016 for Dummies
Author: Greg Harvey
Description: The book covers everything you need to know to perform the task at hand. Includes information on creating and editing worksheets, formatting cells, entering formulas, creating and editing charts, inserting graphs, designing database forms, adding database records, using seek-and-find options, printing, adding hyperlinks to worksheets, saving worksheets as web pages, adding existing worksheet data to an existing webpage and sending worksheets via e-mail.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Filmmaking For Dummies, 3ed
Author: Bryan Michael Stoller
Description: This new edition of Filmmaking For Dummies includes coverage of the latest filmmaking technology, software, apps, and distribution methods and helps aspiring filmmakers learn the ins and outs of financing a film, planning a location, hiring a cast and crew and much more.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Programming Interviews for Dummies
Author: John Sonmez, Eric Butow
Description: Programming Interviews For Dummies will tell you not only the topics and problems you need to know to ace the programming test, but also the interview(s) with other people in the company...and do so under pressure. Additionally, This book will also talk about creating interviews from the interviewer's point of view that will not only help companies prepare to interview programmers but help programmer candidates understand what companies are looking for.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Cybersecurity for Dummies
Author: Joseph Steinberg
Description: Protecting your business and family against cyber attacks! If keeping your business or your family safe from Cybersecurity threats is on your to-do list, Cybersecurity For Dummies will introduce you to the basics of becoming cyber-secure! You will learn what threats exist, and how to identify, protect against, detect, and respond to these threats, as well as how to recover once there has been a breach! This book helps you identify vulnerabilities that may make you a target of cyber-crime and helps you stay on top of your security game.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Microsoft Excel Power Pivot & Power Query For Dummies
Author: Michael Alexander
Description: Want to familiarize yourself with the rich set of Microsoft Excel tools and reporting capabilities available from PowerPivot and Power Query? Look no further! Excel PowerPivot & Power Query For Dummies shows you how this powerful new set of tools can be leveraged to more effectively source and incorporate 'big data' Business Intelligence and Dashboard reports. You'll discover how PowerPivot and Power Query not only allow you to save time and simplify your processes, but also enable you to substantially enhance your data analysis and reporting capabilities.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Cognitive Psychology For Dummies
Author: Peter J. Hills, Michael Pake
Description: Cognitive Psychology For Dummies follows the structure of a typical university course, which makes it the perfect supplement for students in need of a clear and enjoyable overview of the topic. The complexities of a field that explores internal mental processes – including the study of how people perceive, remember, think, speak and solve problems – can be overwhelming for first-year psychology students. This practical resource cuts through the academic-speak to provide a clear understanding of the most important elements of cognitive psychology.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: SEO for Dummies, 7ed
Author: Peter Kent
Description: Create a website that ranks at the top of search engines and drives traffic to your site with these tips, tricks, and secrets. Search Engine Optimization For Dummies, 7th Edition shows website owners, developers, and search engine optimizers (SEOs) how to create a website that ranks at the top of search engines and has high-volume traffic, while answering the essential question of "how do I get people to visit my site?"

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Affiliate Marketing for Dummies
Author: Ted Sudol, Paul Mladjenovic
Description: Amazon, Lowe's, Priceline, Overstock and thousands of large and small companies have affiliate programs. For those thinking of starting an online affiliate marketing venture from the ground up, now is the best time to do this.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Bridge For Dummies, 4ed
Author: Eddie Kantar
Description: Bridge is the most popular card game in the world—and, as any player will tell you, is simply the best card game ever. Whether you're new to the game or a long-time player looking for new tricks, this new edition of Bridge For Dummies walks you through the intricacies of the game and arms you with tried-and-true tips and strategies for being a better player and beating your opponents from the very first draw. Covering not only traditional contract bridge, but other popular variations of the game—including ACOL, Rubber and Duplicate Bridge—this hands-on, friendly guide takes the guesswork out of this beloved game and arms you with the knowledge and know-how to make your game mates your minions.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Account-Based Marketing For Dummies
Author: Sangram Vajre
Description: Account Based Marketing for Dummies will help marketers and sales teams build a coordinated effort to reach specific accounts. Readers will understand how to align the marketing and sales teams to make ABM most effective. They will learn how to analyze current data to identify the accounts with the biggest ROI opportunities, and then the techniques to most effectively reach each account, rather than the individual listed in their CRM for that account. Using online advertising and other messaging in the places the accounts are already engaging online, account based marketers can engage with current and potential customers on their terms.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Salesforce for Dummies, 7ed
Author: Liz Kao, Jon Paz
Description: Salesforce.com For Dummies, 7th Edition gives you an edge in building relationships and managing your company's sales, marketing, customer service, and support operations. You will learn how to maximize the new user interface to organize contacts, schedule business appointments, use forecasting tools to predict upcoming sales, make accurate projects based on past performance, and more. Written by Salesforce.com insiders with years of expertise in CRM services, this new edition covers the latest enhancements to Salesforce.com, the world's most popular customer relationship management software.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Microsoft Excel Sales Forecasting For Dummies, 2ed
Author: Conrad Carlberg
Description: One of the biggest challenges sales and marketing professionals face is forecasting future sales as accurately as possible. The few books published on the subject are full of arcane business school terms and complex algebraic equations. With Microsoft Excel, Conrad Carlberg manages to cut through the jargon and complex math and shows you how to produce forecasts you can rely on.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Digital Marketing For Dummies
Author: Ryan Deiss, Russ Henneberry
Description: Discover how to expand the reach of your brand, acquire and monetize customers, and increase audience engagement with the proven digital marketing strategy and tactics found in this comprehensive guide. Learn how to determine the best product and market fit for your business, measure what your traffic is worth from any source, and offer an effective lead magnet to convert visitors. Use these tools, and more, to change the relationship from prospect to loyal customer.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Product Management For Dummies
Author: Brian Lawley, Pamela Schure
Description: Product management plays a pivotal role in organizations. In fact, it's now considered the fourth most important title in corporate America—yet only a tiny fraction of product managers have been trained for this vital position. If you're one of the hundreds of thousands of people who hold this essential job—or simply aspire to break into a new role—Product Management For Dummies gives you the tools to increase your skill level and manage products like a pro. From defining what product management is—and isn't—to exploring the rising importance of product management in the corporate world, this friendly and accessible guide quickly gets you up to speed on everything it takes to thrive in this growing field.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Employer Branding For Dummies
Author: Richard Mosley, Lars Schmidt
Description: Employer Branding For Dummies is the clear, no-nonsense guide to attracting and retaining top talent. Written by two of the most recognized leaders in employer brand, Richard Mosley and Lars Schmidt, this book gives you actionable advice and expert insight you need to build, scale, and measure a compelling brand. You'll learn how to research what makes your company stand out, the best ways to reach the people you need, and how to convince those people that your company is the ideal place to exercise and develop their skills. The book includes ways to identify the specific traits of your company that aligns with specific talent, and how to translate those traits into employer brand tactic that help you draw the right talent, while repelling the wrong ones.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Java For Dummies, 7ed
Author: Barry Burd
Description: Java For Dummies remains the straightforward reference on Java, covering object-oriented programming basics with Java, code reuse, and the essentials of creating a Java program. The book helps readers create basic Java objects as well as figure out when they can reuse existing code. This edition is updated to cover the changes with the newest version of Java including enhanced multimedia capabilities, updates for Android and cloud coverage and new programming features such as closures, new libraries, new tools, parallel frameworks and more.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Java All-in-One For Dummies, 5ed
Author: Doug Lowe
Description: If you've always wanted to program with Java but didn't know where to start, this will be the java-stained reference you'll turn to again and again. Fully updated for the JDK 9, this deep reference on the world's most popular programming language is the perfect starting point for building things with Java—and an invaluable ongoing reference as you continue to deepen your knowledge. Clocking in at over 900 pages, Java All-in-One For Dummies takes the intimidation out of learning Java and offers clear, step-by-step guidance on how to download and install Java tools; work with variables, numbers, expressions, statements, loops, methods, and exceptions; create applets, servlets, and JavaServer pages; handle and organize data; and so much more.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Trading For Dummies, 4ed
Author: Lita Epstein, Grayson D. Roze
Description: Taking the stress out of the stock market, this no-nonsense guide walks you through all the steps to trade with authority—and takes your portfolio to exciting new heights. Whether you're an investor looking for a clear guide to successfully trading stocks in any type of market, or an investor who has experience trading and are looking for new, proven methods to enhance the profitability of investments, you'll find a proven system for eliminating doubt, decreasing risk, and, ultimately, increasing return.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: 3D Printing For Dummies, 2ed
Author: Richard Horne, Kalani Kirk Hausman
Description: 3D printing is one of the coolest inventions we've seen in our lifetime, and now you can join the ranks of businesspeople, entrepreneurs, and hobbyists who use it to do everything from printing foods and candles to replacement parts for older technologies—and tons of mind-blowing stuff in between! With 3D Printing For Dummies at the helm, you'll find all the fast and easy-to-follow guidance you need to grasp the methods available to create 3D printable objects using software, 3D scanners, and even photographs through open source software applications like 123D Catch.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: CRM For Dummies
Author: Lars Helgeson
Description: CRM For Dummies is the small business leader's guide to managing customer interactions. Customer relationship management is a critical part of any business, and it encompasses everything from business strategy and HR to sales, marketing, events, and more. Solutions exist for businesses of any size, but how do you know which one is right for you? What features do you need? Do you have the people and processes in place to get the most out of whichever one you choose? This book is designed to help business leaders better understand effective CRM and identify the right solution for their business—but it's about much more than software; effective CRM requires appropriate team structures, intradepartmental collaboration, and process efficiency.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: OpenOffice.org For Dummies
Author: Gurdy Leete, Ellen Finkelstein, Mary Leete
Description: OpenOffice.org For Dummies shows Linux and Windows users how to use the OpenOffice (OO) desktop productivity suite in the For Dummies friendly, easy-to-understand style. This book explains how to download, install, and set up both the Linux and Windows versions of OO. The book is organized by the four key desktop applications provided in the OO suite: Writer (word processing product), Calc (spreadsheet product), Impress (presentation product), and Draw (graphics product). Each part focuses on one of the applications and explains how to use it and work with it on the Web.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Business Coaching & Mentoring For Dummies, 2ed
Author: Marie Taylor, Steve Crabb
Description: Business Coaching & Mentoring For Dummies provides business owners and managers with the insight they need to successfully develop the next generation of leaders. Packed with business-led strategies, key concepts, and effective techniques, this book equips you with the skills to transform both yourself and your team. Whether you're coaching colleagues, employees, or offering your skills as a service, these techniques will help you build a productive relationship that leads to business success. The companion website also features eight bonus videos that will further your mastery by showing you what great coaching looks like in action. Navigate tricky situations and emotional minefields with ease; develop vision, values, and a mission; create a long-term plan—everything you need is here, wit

Subject: For Dummies
Title: AWS for Developers For Dummies
Author: John Paul Mueller
Description: Modern businesses rely on Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS)—a setup in which someone else foots the bill to create application environments—and developers are expected to know how to write both platform-specific and IaaS-supported applications. If you're a developer who writes desktop and web applications but have little-to-no experience with cloud development, this book is an essential tool in getting started in the IaaS environment with Amazon Web Services.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Salesforce Marketing Cloud For Dummies
Author: Chester Bullock, Mark Pollard
Description: Salesforce Marketing Cloud For Dummies will help you make the most of your investment in this exciting digital marketing suite of products. Use Salesforce Marketing Cloud to personalize your customers' journey, whether they are visiting you on the web, via their mobile device, or on Facebook. Discover how you can set-up and establish the data sets that are most important to your job function, use the robust e-mail tool for your direct marketing campaign, engage your customers on mobile devices, and use predictive intelligence to achieve optimal engagement across the customer journey.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: C# 7.0 All-in-One For Dummies
Author: John Paul Mueller, Bill Sempf, Chuck Sphar
Description: C# know-how is a must if you want to be a professional Microsoft developer. It's also good to know a little C# if you're building tools for the web, mobile apps, or other development tasks. This reference offers a deep dive into C# for coders still learning the nuances of the valuable programming language. Pop it open to get an intro into coding with C#, how to design secure apps and databases, and even pointers on building web and mobile apps with C#.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Beginning Programming with Python For Dummies, 2ed
Author: John Paul Mueller
Description: Python is a remarkably powerful and dynamic programming language that's used in a wide variety of application domains. Some of its key distinguishing features include a very clear, readable syntax, strong introspection capabilities, intuitive object orientation, and natural expression of procedural code. Plus, Python features full modularity, supporting hierarchical packages, exception-based error handling, and modules easily written in C, C++, Java, R, or .NET languages, such as C#. In addition, Python supports a number of coding styles that include: functional, imperative, object-oriented, and procedural.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Excel Macros For Dummies, 2ed
Author: Michael Alexander
Description: Looking for ready-made Excel macros that will streamline your workflow? Look no further! Excel Macros For Dummies, 2 nd Edition helps you save time, automate, and be more productive–even with no programming experience at all. Each chapter offers macros you can implement right away, with practical exercises that extend your knowledge and help you understand the mechanics at work. You'll find over 70 of the most effective Excel macros for solving common problems and explanations of why and where to use each one–plus invaluable guidance and step-by-step instruction for applying them effectively.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Adobe Creative Cloud All-in-One For Dummies, 2ed
Author: Jennifer Smith, Christopher Smith
Description: Adobe Creative Cloud is the most popular suite of tools among creative professionals, and a valuable resource you can use to fulfill all of your design goals. Ready to get started? The only book on the market of its kind, Adobe Creative Cloud All-in-One For Dummies is written by designers for designers. It will provide you with expert instruction that spans seven mini-books, with helpful information that can grant insight regardless of your current level of knowledge.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: R Projects For Dummies
Author: Joseph Schmuller
Description: R Projects For Dummies offers a unique learn-by-doing approach. You will increase the depth and breadth of your R skillset by completing a wide variety of projects. By using R’s graphics, interactive, and machine learning tools, you’ll learn to apply R’s extensive capabilities in an array of scenarios. The depth of the project experience is unmatched by any other content online or in print. And you just might increase your statistics knowledge along the way, too!

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Scrum For Dummies, 2ed
Author: Mark C. Layton, David Morrow
Description: Scrum is an agile project management framework that allows for flexibility and collaboration to be a part of your workflow. Primarily used by software developers, scrum can be used across many job functions and industries. Scrum can also be used in your personal life to help you plan for retirement, a trip, or even a wedding or other big event. Scrum provides a small set of rules that create just enough structure for teams to be able to focus their innovation on solving what might otherwise be an insurmountable challenge. Scrum For Dummies shows you how to assemble a scrum taskforce and use it to implement this popular Agile methodology to make projects in your professional and personal life run more smoothly—from start to finish.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: PHP, MySQL & JavaScript All-in-One For Dummies
Author: Richard Blum
Description: It takes a powerful suite of technologies to drive the most-visited websites in the world. PHP, mySQL, JavaScript, and other web-building languages serve as the foundation for application development and programming projects at all levels of the web. Dig into this all-in-one book to get a grasp on these in-demand skills, and figure out how to apply them to become a professional web builder. You’ll get valuable information from seven handy books covering the pieces of web programming, HTML5 & CSS3, JavaScript, PHP, MySQL, creating object-oriented programs, and using PHP frameworks.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Web Coding & Development All-in-One For Dummies
Author: Paul McFedries
Description: It requires a lot of coding know-how to build the interfaces, databases, and other features that run modern websites, web apps, and mobile apps. This book serves as an interpreter for the languages that handle those tasks. Start with a refesher on the rules of coding before diving into the languages that build interfaces, add interactivity to the web, or store and deliver data to sites. When you're ready, jump into guidance on how to put it all together to build a site or create an app.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Enterprise Agility For Dummies
Author: Doug Rose
Description: An enterprise agile transformation is a radical organizational change, and this book can help you manage that change. A successful transformation depends on understanding your organization's culture, and choosing the right enterprise agile framework based on that culture. You'll also get ideas on how to put together a change management plan that will earn you organizational buy in and support.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Instagram For Business For Dummies
Author: Jenn Herman, Eric Butow, Corey Walker
Description: Instagram is the photo- and video-sharing app used by millions across the globe. More than just a social platform for users to share their experiences with family and friends, it's become a vital tool for business owners and marketers to create visual narratives about what makes them, and their products, different from everything else that's out there. Instagram For Business For Dummies shows you how to use the app to connect with your audience in a meaningful way as you showcase your products and offer a unique insider's view of your brand. Perfect for Instagram newbies or those who want to spruce up an existing account, this book helps you set up a powerful business profile, compose successful posts, and tell your story to the world.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Hacking For Dummies, 6ed
Author: Kevin Beaver
Description: Hacking For Dummies, 6th Edition gives insight into the hacker's mind so that you can protect your data and your system from a variety of attacks. With this book, you will learn how to recognize the vulnerabilities in your systems so you can protect them more diligently. Expert author Kevin Beaver shares his knowledge on penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, security best practices, and every aspect of ethical hacking that is essential in order to protect your data and your system.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Increasing Your Influence at Work All-In-One For Dummies
Author: Christina Tangora Schlachter
Description: Influence is a timeless topic for business leaders and others in positions of power, but the world has evolved to the point where everyone needs these skills. No matter your job, role, rank, or function, if you want to get things done you need to know how to influence up, down, across, and outside the organization. Increasing Your Influence at Work All-in-One For Dummies shows you how to contribute more fully to important decisions, resolve conflicts more easily, lead and manage more effectively, and much more. Plus, you'll discover how to develop the most important attributes necessary for influence—trustworthiness, reliability, and assertiveness—and find out how to move beyond.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Linux All-In-One For Dummies, 6ed
Author: Emmett Dulaney
Description: This new edition of Linux All-in-One For Dummies has a unique focus on Ubuntu, while still including coverage of Debian, Red Hat, SuSE, and others. The market is looking for administrators, and part of the qualifications needed for job openings is the authentication of skills by vendor-neutral third parties (CompTIA/Linux Professional Institute)—and that’s something other books out there don’t address.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Microsoft Office 2019 For Dummies
Author: Wallace Wang
Description: Microsoft Office basics including typing and formatting text in Word, designing your Word documents, navigating an Excel spreadsheet, editing a spreadsheet, creating formulas, charting and analyzing data, creating a PowerPoint presentation, adding color, pictures, and sound to a presentation, preparing your presentation for an audience, configuring e-mail with Outlook, storing contacts and organizing tasks, scheduling your time and setting appointments, designing an Access database, editing and modifying a database, searching, sorting, and querying a database, creating a database report, and more. Information is presented in the straightforward but fun language that has defined the Dummies series for more than twenty years.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Gamification Marketing For Dummies
Author: Zarrar Chishti
Description: Nothing drives interest from customers more than giving them the chance to win. From old school sweepstakes and stamp collecting games to modern mobile loyalty programs or branded game apps, gamification helps connect you to your customers in a way they invite into their lives. This book helps you add the effective but challenging aspects of gamification to your marketing strategy. No matter what your digital proficiency, Intelligent Marketing with Gamification For Dummies will guide both veteran and inexperienced marketers through the maze of planning and executing a gamification strategy.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: CCSP For Dummies with Online Practice
Author: Arthur J. Deane
Description: If you are a cloud security professional seeking your CSSP certification, this book is a perfect way to prepare for the exam. Covering in detail all six domains, the expert advice in this book gives you key information you'll need to pass the exam. In addition to the information covered on the exam, you'll get tips on setting up a study plan, tips for exam day, and access to an online test bank of questions.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Lean Six Sigma for Dummies, 3ed
Author: John Morgan , Martin Brenig-Jones
Description: Are you looking to make your organisation more effective and productive? If you answered "yes," you need to change the way it thinks. Combining the leading improvement methods of Six Sigma and Lean, this winning technique drives performance to the next level—and this friendly and accessible guide shows you how. The third edition of Lean Six Sigma For Dummies outlines the key concepts of this strategy and explains how you can use it to get the very best out of your team and your business.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Mindfulness for Dummies, 3ed
Author: Shamash Alidina
Description: Are you tired, unproductive or stressed out? Maybe you're suffering from high-blood pressure, illness or even chronic pain? Recommended by medical professionals throughout the UK, mindfulness helps to clear the mind of the distracting thoughts and mental clutter that accumulate day-to-day. Focusing on breathing and other self-control techniques, mindfulness is scientifically proven to leave patients calmer, more self-aware, and free from the unrealistic expectations that damage the way we all live and work. And with this expert, easy-to-follow guide, there's never been a better time to get to grips with mindfulness and the many ways it can help you lead a happier, healthier life.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy for Dummies, 3ed
Author: Rob Willson, Rhena Branch
Description: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy For Dummies, 3rd Edition will help readers identify negative and unhealthy patterns of thinking which have been holding them back from the changes they want. CBT can help readers overcome anxiety and depression, boost self-esteem, lose weight, manage anger, beat addiction or simply improve their outlook in both their professional and personal lives. The straightforward and practical advice will help readers apply CBT principles to their own lives, whatever issues they'd like to work on.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Social Media Marketing for Dummies, 4ed
Author: Shiv Singh, Stephanie Diamond
Description: Social Media Marketing For Dummies, 4th Edition presents the essence of planning, launching, managing, and assessing a social marketing campaign in an economic 350-page guide - perfect for time-pressed marketers. This edition brings the existing content up to date for 2019 and adds new content as well.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Data Science Programming All - in - One for Dummies
Author: John Paul Mueller, Luca Massaron
Description: Most of the books on the market are written for the science professional and often limit their audience to a reader specializing in data science, AI, or computer science. These books focus on people who already have the coding knowledge to write applications, the math background to work with algorithms, and the data management background to interact with data sources successfully. This book addresses their needs by providing simple techniques to access data, manipulate it using clearly described algorithms, and interacting with it using code written in one of three languages. The result is a book that has a broad audience of people who need this resource.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Python All - in - One For Dummies
Author: John Shovic, Alan Simpson
Description: There's almost no type of project that Python can't make better. From creating apps to building complex web sites to sorting big data, Python provides a way to get the work done. Python All-in-One For Dummies offers a starting point for those new to coding by explaining the basics of Python. Experienced coders looking for more than the basics can also find how Python can be applied to projects in the enterprise, including data analysis and other major undertakings.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Ai For Gig Workers: Essential Strategies To Transform Your Freelance Career
Author: Savage
Description: AI for Gig Workers is a practical guide for freelancers, contractors, and gig workers looking to use AI tools to grow their income, improve productivity, and simplify everyday work challenges. Covering personal branding, automation, prompt writing, scam prevention, and AI-powered workflow management, the book provides hands-on strategies to help independent professionals thrive in the modern gig economy.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Bootstrap Empire: The Bala Blueprint For Building A $100M Brand From The Ground Up
Author: Holloway

Drawing from her experience growing Bala into a multimillion-dollar fitness company, Natalie Holloway shares actionable strategies for bootstrapping, branding, marketing, hiring, pitching investors, and scaling a business with limited resources.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Mastering Gold And Silver Markets: Insights From A Legendary Bullion Bank Trader
Author: Gottlieb

Blending market analysis with real-world trading insights, the book explains gold and silver pricing, futures and OTC markets, arbitrage strategies, supply-demand dynamics, and major market disruptions, making it a valuable guide for investors, traders, and finance professionals.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: 100 Questions & Answers About Social Security For Dummies
Author: Experts At Aarp

Organized in a practical Q&A format, the book helps readers confidently navigate the complexities of the Social Security system and make informed financial decisions.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Leading With Strategy: Using Your North Star To Guide Decision-Making
Author: Tiryaki
Description: Leading With Strategy is a practical leadership guide that helps managers and executives navigate complex business decisions in today’s AI-driven world. Featuring 50 visual frameworks and decision-making models, Timothy Tiryaki provides actionable tools for strategic thinking, problem-solving, collaboration, and leadership execution across a wide range of workplace challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Persuasion Engine: How Any Business Can Use Ai-Powered Neuromarketing To Understand And Win Customers
Author: Dooley
Description: The Persuasion Engine explores how AI-powered neuromarketing and behavioural science can help businesses better understand customer decision-making and improve marketing performance. Roger Dooley provides practical strategies for using tools like eye tracking, biometrics, implicit testing, and AI-driven behavioural analysis to increase engagement, boost conversions, and create more persuasive customer experiences.

Subject: General
Title: Transforming Community Health With Psychology And Ai
Author: Armagan Bogatek
Description: Transforming Community Health with Psychology and AI explores how artificial intelligence can support healthier and more equitable communities through culturally sensitive, human-centered healthcare solutions. Combining psychology, public health, and AI, the book presents practical frameworks and real-world case studies on using technology to improve mental well-being, reduce health disparities, and strengthen community-based healthcare systems.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Computational Intelligence In Biomedical Internet Of Medical Things
Author: Prakash Yadav
Description: Biomedical Internet of Medical Things explores how AI, machine learning, deep learning, and computational intelligence are transforming healthcare through intelligent medical devices and connected health systems. Covering topics such as wearable sensors, predictive analytics, real-time diagnostics, personalized treatment, remote monitoring, and healthcare cybersecurity, the book provides researchers and professionals with a comprehensive guide to modern biomedical IoMT technologies and applications.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Outrageous Startup Growth: Uncovering The Secrets Of User Psychology To Scale Your Success
Author: Hodge
Description: Outrageous Startup Growth reveals how behavioral psychology and growth strategies can help startups achieve rapid and sustainable success. Drawing on his experience scaling tech companies to over 100 million users, Colin Hodge shares practical frameworks for improving user acquisition, retention, engagement, branding, and customer loyalty through mindset development and psychology-driven product design.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Beyond Distinction: How Leaders Transcend The Turbulence Of An Ai-Transformed World
Author: Mckain
Description: Beyond Distinction explores how leaders can build organizations that achieve lasting competitive advantage in an AI-driven business world. Scott McKain provides practical strategies for creating meaningful customer relationships, attracting top talent, overcoming competitor imitation, and embedding distinction into company culture to drive long-term growth and relevance.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Working Scared: Blueprints For Employees And Leaders To Succeed During Turbulent Times
Author: Silverman
Description: Working Scared provides practical strategies for employees and leaders navigating workplace uncertainty in today’s rapidly changing business environment. Covering challenges such as AI transformation, hybrid work, economic instability, restructuring, and difficult leadership, the book combines research, real-world case studies, and actionable frameworks to help professionals build resilience, adaptability, and long-term career success.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Membrane Technology For Environmental Remediation - From Principles To Applied Solutions
Author: Shen
Description: Membrane Technology for Environmental Remediation provides a comprehensive guide to advanced membrane science and its applications in water treatment, pollution control, and sustainable resource recovery. Covering membrane design, AI-enabled system optimization, anti-fouling strategies, catalytic and smart membranes, and scalable remediation technologies, the book equips engineers, researchers, and environmental professionals with practical solutions for modern environmental challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Brainstorm: A Guide To Neurodivergent Talent And The Future Of Work
Author: Thompson
Description: Brainstorm: A Guide to Neurodivergent Talent and the Future of Work explores how organizations can create more inclusive, innovative, and high-performing workplaces by embracing neurodiversity. Combining practical strategies, real-world insights, and workplace frameworks, Dave Thompson shows leaders and teams how to rethink hiring, management, communication, and workplace culture to unlock the full potential of neurodivergent talent.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Cheers To Monday: The Surprisingly Simple Method To Un-Stress Your Work And Transform Your Life
Author: Leneker
Description: Cheers to Monday challenges the modern culture of stress and burnout by showing that chronic stress is not a sign of success, but a barrier to wellbeing and performance. Drawing on leadership coaching, real-world experience, and science-backed strategies, Amy Leneker provides practical tools to help professionals reduce stress, regain energy and focus, and create healthier, more joyful approaches to work and leadership.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Great Leaders Care: Building Psychologically Safe And Resilient Teams
Author: Cowan
Description: Great Leaders Care presents a practical and evidence-based approach to leadership that prioritizes wellbeing, resilience, and high performance. Drawing on leadership research, mental health advocacy, and real-world executive insights, Graeme Cowan shows leaders how to build caring workplace cultures, prevent burnout, strengthen psychological safety, and create teams that thrive both personally and professionally.

Subject: General
Title: Good Girl, Goodbye: Rewrite Your Story, Reclaim Your Power, Build A Life You Love
Author: Judd
Description: Good Girl, Goodbye empowers women to break free from people-pleasing, perfectionism, and the pressure to always be “good.” Drawing on positive psychology, coaching insights, and real-life experiences, Caitlin Judd provides practical tools to help readers set boundaries, reclaim confidence, define success on their own terms, and build a more authentic and fulfilling life.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Passive Income For Dummies
Author: Mladjenovic
Description: Passive Income For Dummies provides practical, beginner-friendly strategies for building multiple streams of passive income through investments, digital products, affiliate marketing, subscriptions, and other income-generating opportunities. Drawing on decades of experience, Paul Mladjenovic offers step-by-step guidance, automation tips, and risk-management advice to help readers create greater financial security and long-term wealth.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Data Science First: Using Language Models In Ai-Enabled Applications
Author: Hawkins
Description: Data Science First explores how data scientists can effectively integrate language models into real-world AI applications while maintaining reliability, accuracy, and business value. Combining practical workflows, implementation patterns, case studies, and evaluation methods, John Hawkins demonstrates how generative AI can enhance traditional data science across industries such as banking, telecom, education, and insurance.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Warren Buffett Ceo: Secrets From The Berkshire Hathaway Managers, 25Th Anniversary Edition
Author: Miles
Description: The Warren Buffett CEO, 25th Anniversary Edition, offers an in-depth look at the evolution of Berkshire Hathaway, exploring its extraordinary growth, leadership culture, and succession from Warren Buffett to Greg Abel. Through executive insights, business case studies, and lessons from Berkshire’s insurance, railroad, energy, and manufacturing operations, the book reveals the strategies, leadership principles, and culture behind one of the world’s most successful conglomerates.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Data Makes The World Go 'Round: The Data, Tech, And Trust Behind Ai Success
Author: Halper
Description: Data Makes the World Go ‘Round provides a practical roadmap for organizations seeking to implement AI successfully through strong data, technology, and governance foundations. Combining real-world case studies, leadership insights, and actionable strategies, Fern Halper explains how businesses can build scalable data architectures, operationalize AI, manage risk and ethics, and align AI initiatives with organizational goals and measurable outcomes.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Making Ai Work For People - A Framework For Designing And Building Impactful Ai-Powered Applications
Author: Ibrahim
Description: Making AI Work for People presents a human-centered framework for designing AI applications that are ethical, trustworthy, and impactful. Through the PRESS framework, Asmaa Ibrahim guides software engineers, product managers, and designers in building AI-powered solutions that prioritize people, explainability, safety, responsibility, and long-term value while solving real-world business and user challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Leading Beyond Fear: The Formula For Psychological Safety, Adaptability, And Growth
Author: Pretty
Description: Leading Beyond Fear is a practical leadership guide focused on building psychological safety, strong workplace culture, and high-performing teams. Neil Pretty provides actionable strategies to help leaders develop confidence, influence, adaptability, and authentic leadership skills while fostering trust, collaboration, innovation, and growth in modern organizations.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Untangling Ai - Driving Business Success Through Enterprise Automation And Ai Agents
Author: Kesby
Description: Untangling AI provides a practical roadmap for organizations looking to adopt AI and enterprise automation at scale. Matt Kesby explains how leaders can integrate AI agents into business operations, automate workflows, build AI-ready teams, and create effective strategies around technology, execution, ethics, and communication to drive long-term business transformation and efficiency.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Valuable And Visible: Redefining Personal Branding By Leading With Impact Over Image
Author: Errecarte
Description: Valuable & Visible redefines personal branding by showing professionals how to stand out through clarity, contribution, and memorable ideas rather than self-promotion. Vanessa Errecarte provides practical strategies to help readers build trust, communicate expertise effectively, create meaningful visibility, and become recognized for a distinct point of view that attracts opportunities and long-term success.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Warren Buffett Portfolio: Mastering The Power Of The Focus Investment Strategy, 25Th Anniversary Edition, Wiley Investment Classics
Author: Hagstrom
Description: The Warren Buffett Portfolio, 25th Anniversary Edition, explores the principles of focus investing through the proven strategies of Warren Buffett and insights from Charlie Munger. Robert G. Hagstrom combines investment psychology, probability theory, market behavior, and practical portfolio management to show investors how concentrated, business-driven investing can create long-term wealth and outperform traditional diversification strategies.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Good Work: Transform Your Work From The Inside Out
Author: Page
Description: Good Work explores how modern workplaces can move beyond burnout, disconnection, and outdated work systems to create healthier, more meaningful environments. Drawing on psychological research and real-world examples, Dr. Kat Page provides practical strategies for building connected teams, improving workplace wellbeing, fostering honest communication, and creating sustainable cultures where people and organizations can thrive together.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: The Pricing Playbook: A Practical Guide To Revenue Growth Management In The Age Of Ai
Author: Reinhardt
Description: The Pricing Playbook provides a practical framework for building effective pricing and revenue growth strategies in the age of AI. Ingo Reinhardt combines pricing theory, behavioral economics, and real-world case studies to help business leaders understand value pricing, price elasticity, and AI-driven decision-making while creating data-informed strategies that drive sustainable growth and profitability.

Subject: General
Title: Aquatic Pollution: An Introductory Text
Author: Laws
Description: Aquatic Pollution: An Introductory Text, Fifth Edition, provides a comprehensive overview of pollution affecting freshwater and marine ecosystems. Covering topics such as climate change impacts, ocean acidification, persistent organic pollutants, nutrient loading, oil spills, and urban runoff, the book combines updated research, real-world case studies, and environmental science fundamentals to help students, researchers, policymakers, and practitioners understand and address aquatic pollution challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Robot-Proof: When Machines Have All The Answers, Build Better People
Author: Ming
Description: Robot-Proof explores how people, businesses, and societies can thrive in an AI-driven world by focusing on uniquely human strengths. Dr. Vivienne Ming combines insights from artificial intelligence, psychology, and human behavior to examine the future of work, education, teamwork, and problem-solving, showing how adaptability, creativity, and human-centered thinking can help individuals remain valuable in an increasingly automated future.

Subject: General
Title: Laser Materials Processing And Manufacturing Techniques
Author: Bahadur
Description: Laser Materials Processing provides a comprehensive introduction to the principles, technologies, and industrial applications of laser-based manufacturing. Covering techniques such as cutting, welding, drilling, surface treatment, micromachining, and additive manufacturing, the book explores laser-material interactions, process optimization, and real-world industrial use cases, making it a valuable resource for engineers, researchers, and students in manufacturing and industrial engineering.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Adversarial Machine Learning: Mechanisms, Vulnerabilities, And Strategies For Trustworthy Ai
Author: Edwards
Description: Adversarial Machine Learning is a practical and comprehensive guide to securing AI systems against modern adversarial threats. Covering the full machine learning lifecycle, the book explains attacks such as evasion, poisoning, backdoor, privacy, and generative AI exploits, while also exploring defence strategies including adversarial training, certified defences, and privacy-preserving techniques. Designed for developers, security professionals, and AI leaders, it provides both technical depth and real-world guidance for building resilient and trustworthy AI systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: A Leader Worth Following: How To Master Yourself, Influence Others, And Create Exceptional Experiences
Author: Granger
Description: A Leader Worth Following presents a modern, human-centered approach to leadership built on emotional intelligence, empathy, communication, and psychological insight. Dr. Benjamin Granger combines workplace psychology and experience management research to help leaders build trust, strengthen relationships, improve influence, and inspire high performance through compassionate and effective leadership practices.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Capitalism For All: Inclusive Economics And The Future-Proofing Of America
Author: Bryant
Description: Capitalism For All explores how inclusive economics can rebuild prosperity, strengthen the middle class, and future-proof society in an era of rapid technological change. Drawing on decades of experience in financial empowerment and community development, John Hope Bryant presents practical strategies for expanding economic opportunity, leveraging AI and technology responsibly, and creating a more equitable form of capitalism that benefits businesses, communities, and society as a whole.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: The Ai Illusion: Why Machines Aren'T Creative
Author: Julia
Description: The AI Illusion examines the realities and limitations of generative AI, challenging the hype surrounding machine creativity and artificial general intelligence. Drawing on decades of experience in the tech industry, Luc Julia explains how AI systems work, why they rely on recombining existing data rather than true understanding, and the societal, economic, and environmental impacts of AI adoption while advocating for more responsible and realistic approaches to AI development.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Humanity?S Edge: How Self-Leadership And Accountability Build High-Performing Teams In The Age Of Ai
Author: Solanki
Description: Humanity’s Edge explores how self-leadership, accountability, and authenticity can help leaders build high-performing teams in an AI-driven workplace. Drawing on psychology, executive coaching, and real-world business experience, Baiju Solanki provides practical strategies for developing self-awareness, empowering teams, strengthening workplace culture, and leading confidently through hybrid work and technological change.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Managing Operational Risk In A Changing World
Author: Cagan
Description: Managing Operational Risk in a Changing World provides a practical guide to addressing modern operational risks in an increasingly complex business environment. Combining decades of industry experience with real-world case studies and expert interviews, the book explores emerging challenges such as cyber threats, AI, ESG, climate risk, geopolitical events, and evolving regulations, offering actionable frameworks and strategies for risk professionals, students, and business leaders.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Artificial Intelligence And Computational Modeling In Heat Transfer And Fluid Dynamics
Author: Awasthi
Description: Artificial Intelligence in Heat Transfer and Fluid Dynamics explores how AI and machine learning are transforming thermal sciences by enhancing traditional computational methods used in heat transfer and fluid dynamics. Covering topics such as AI-driven optimisation, neural network modelling, predictive maintenance, turbulence analysis, microfluidics, and real-time flow control, the book combines theory, practical applications, and industrial case studies to help engineers and researchers solve complex thermal and fluid engineering challenges more efficiently.

Subject: General
Title: Immigration: How The Past Shapes The Present
Author: Foner
Description: Immigration: How the Past Shapes the Present examines how America’s immigration history continues to influence modern immigration debates, policies, and social attitudes. Drawing on historical and contemporary research, the book explores migration patterns, public perceptions, and the roots of modern nativism, offering valuable insights into how past immigration experiences shape present-day society and future challenges.

Subject: Economics
Title: Beyond Generation Rent: Political Economy, Inequality, And The Private Rental Sector
Author: Byrne
Description: Beyond Generation Rent explores the growing inequalities within modern housing systems and the global rise of private renting. Drawing on international research and case studies, Michael Byrne examines issues such as financialization, landlord power, evictions, declining homeownership, and housing insecurity, while highlighting the political and social challenges shaping the future of housing and tenant rights.

Subject: Economics
Title: Beyond Generation Rent: Political Economy, Inequality, And The Private Rental Sector
Author: Byrne
Description: Beyond Generation Rent provides a critical examination of the inequalities shaping modern housing systems and the global growth of private renting. Through international case studies and political-economic analysis, Michael Byrne explores landlordism, housing financialization, evictions, declining homeownership, and tenant activism, highlighting the urgent challenges of housing justice and affordability in contemporary societies.

Subject: General
Title: The German-Russian Century - History Of A Tangled Relationship
Author: Creuzberger
Description: The German-Russian Century explores the complex and deeply influential relationship between Germany and Russia throughout the twentieth century and beyond. Historian Stefan Creuzberger examines how revolution, war, fascism, communism, the Cold War, and modern geopolitical tensions shaped both nations and the wider world, offering a detailed historical analysis of one of the most consequential international relationships in modern history.

Subject: General
Title: Women'S Liberation: Gender Inequality From Suffrage To Austerity
Author: Pat Thane
Description: Women’s Liberation examines the long struggle for gender equality in Britain and the social, political, and economic forces that have shaped it over the past century. Through issues such as employment, education, political representation, health, and violence against women, Pat Thane explores how gender inequality intersects with class, race, disability, and income, offering a thoughtful analysis of both the progress achieved and the challenges that remain.

Subject: General
Title: The Great Realignment: Why The New Right Is Here To Stay
Author: Davies
Description: The Politics of Realignment explores the global political shifts reshaping modern democracies, from the rise of the hard right to the weakening of traditional political alliances. Steve Davies argues that politics is moving away from class- and economy-based divisions toward conflicts centered on national identity, sovereignty, and cosmopolitanism, offering a wide-ranging analysis of the forces transforming political systems across countries such as the USA, Germany, Argentina, and India.

Subject: General
Title: Same Player Shoots Again - A Biography Of The Pinball Machine
Author: Bernard
Description: The Age of Pinball reflects on the rise and disappearance of pinball machines as symbols of a lost era of public entertainment and shared leisure culture. Blending personal memories with cultural analysis, Andreas Bernard explores how changing technology, digital entertainment, and the blurring of work and leisure have transformed social spaces, gaming, and the meaning of free time in modern life.

Subject: General
Title: The Unknowable Body
Author: Moore
Description: The Unknowable Body is a deeply personal and reflective exploration of illness, identity, and the limits of medical understanding. Drawing from her experiences with cancer treatment and family caregiving, Lisa Jean Moore examines how bodies resist clear definition through themes of medical uncertainty, gender, emotion, technology, and embodiment, offering a thoughtful perspective on what it means to live in bodies that are constantly changing and never fully knowable.

Subject: General
Title: Speech Begins After Death: In Conversation With Claude Bonnefoy
Author: Foucault
Description: Speech Begins after Death presents a rare and personal conversation between philosopher Michel Foucault and critic Claude Bonnefoy about Foucault’s relationship with writing, memory, and intellectual life. Through this reflective and autobiographical dialogue, Foucault discusses his literary influences, personal history, and the deeper meaning of writing, offering unique insight into the mind and creative process of one of the twentieth century’s most influential thinkers.

Subject: General
Title: Prompt Thinking: A Critique Of Generative Reason
Author: Xun
Description: Prompt Thinking examines how generative AI is reshaping human thought and intellectual practice. Blending philosophy, technology, and critical reflection, the book explores prompting as a new form of dialogue with artificial intelligence, offering a thoughtful framework for engaging with AI in ways that expand creativity, awareness, and critical thinking while preserving human autonomy.

Subject: Physics
Title: Towards A Unification Of The Laws Of Physics In Classical Fields Theory
Author: Caltagirone
Description: Towards a Unification of the Laws of Physics in Classical Fields Theory presents a theoretical framework aimed at unifying the fundamental laws of physics through discrete mechanics. Revisiting concepts from classical mechanics, relativity, and electromagnetism, the book proposes a single law of motion based on local reference frames, discrete time, and proper acceleration, offering a new perspective on understanding physical phenomena across multiple fields of physics.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Generalized Fractional Brownian Motion
Author: Zili
Description: Generalized Fractional Brownian Motion introduces the Zili generalized fractional Brownian motion (ZgfBm), an advanced stochastic process designed to model complex random phenomena with greater flexibility than traditional fractional models. Covering foundational theory, parameter estimation, stochastic calculus, and applications to stochastic equations, the book provides a rigorous and comprehensive framework for researchers and students working in probability, mathematics, and stochastic analysis.

Subject: General
Title: Scaling Laws And Urban Systems
Author: Vacchiani-Marcu
