It examines how industries such as shipping, trucking, automotive production, and logistics are increasingly vulnerable to cyberattacks, ransomware, and disruptions that can impact global supply chains and public safety. The text presents a risk-based cybersecurity framework that organizations can use to strengthen security management, protect communication systems, and improve resilience across dynamic vendor ecosystems. Combining practical strategies with insights into emerging threats, the book focuses on safeguarding passengers, cargo, transportation networks, and critical infrastructure while helping organizations develop stronger and more effective cybersecurity programs.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Integrated Devices For Artificial Intelligence And Vlsi - Vlsi Design, Simulation And Applications
Author: Raj
Description: This book explores the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) with Very Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) design, highlighting how AI techniques are transforming semiconductor and computer system development. It explains the role of advanced CAD tools such as Synopsys and Mentor Graphics in automating VLSI design processes, while also discussing the limitations of traditional automation approaches. The book focuses on how AI-driven methods, including expert systems and knowledge-based techniques, improve efficiency and problem-solving across various stages of VLSI design such as RTL design, physical design, and static timing analysis (STA). Combining concepts from microelectronics, semiconductor technology, and AI, the text provides insights into emerging opportunities and modern practices shaping the future of the VLSI industry.

Subject: general
Title: Microwave Photonics
Author: Yao
Description: Microwave Photonics provides a comprehensive study of the technologies and techniques that combine microwave engineering with photonics for advanced communication and signal processing applications. Covering developments from the past three decades, the book explains topics such as photonic generation, processing, and distribution of microwave and ultra-wideband (UWB) signals, beamforming for phased array antennas, microwave frequency measurement, analog-to-digital conversion, and quantum microwave photonics. It also discusses important components and concepts including optical fibers, modulators, photodetectors, fiber Bragg gratings, opto-electronic oscillators, and radio-over-fiber networks. Designed for graduate students and researchers, the book offers both theoretical foundations and practical insights into modern microwave photonic systems and their applications in communication, sensing, and signal processing technologies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Chemical Engineering Analysis And Optimization Using Matlab
Author: Xie
Description: Chemical Engineering Analysis and Optimization Using MATLAB® introduces modern computer-aided analysis and optimization techniques for chemical engineering using MATLAB. The book explains how optimization methods are applied in engineering design and manufacturing to improve efficiency, reduce resource use, and maximize performance. It covers programming, mathematical modeling, simulation, and optimization techniques through practical examples, case studies, and MATLAB code implementations. Combining theoretical concepts with hands-on applications, the text helps students and engineering professionals develop valuable skills in process analysis, computational problem-solving, and engineering design optimization.

Subject: computer science
Title: Reinforcement Learning For Cyber Operations: Applications Of Artificial Intelligence For Penetration Testing
Author: Rahman
Description: Reinforcement Learning for Cyber Operations: Applications of Artificial Intelligence for Penetration Testing explores how reinforcement learning (RL) and artificial intelligence can be applied to modern cybersecurity operations and penetration testing. The book explains concepts such as attack path analysis, sensor placement, automated penetration testing, and network defense strategies using reinforcement learning models, Markov Decision Processes, and multi-agent techniques. Through simulated and real-world cybersecurity scenarios, it demonstrates how AI can improve both offensive and defensive cyber operations by identifying vulnerabilities, predicting adversarial behavior, and strengthening organizational security posture. Designed for cybersecurity professionals, researchers, network administrators, and information security teams, the book provides both theoretical foundations and practical applications of AI-driven cyber defense and penetration testing methodologies.

Subject: science
Title: The Advanced Practitioner In Pathophysiology And Diagnostics
Author: Phipps
Description: The Advanced Practitioner in Pathophysiology and Diagnostics is a comprehensive guide designed to help healthcare professionals develop a deeper understanding of disease processes and clinical diagnostics. The book explains how functional changes in the body occur due to illness or injury and shows how practitioners can use patient history, physical examinations, diagnostic tests, and evidence-based approaches to make informed clinical decisions. Covering topics such as differential diagnosis, pathophysiological mechanisms, and diagnostic reasoning, it provides a multidisciplinary and patient-centered perspective suitable for advanced practice healthcare professionals and postgraduate students studying advanced clinical practice and pathophysiology.

Subject: science
Title: How To Read A Paper, The Basics Of Evidence-Based Healthcare, Seventh Edition
Author: Greenhalgh
Description: How to Read a Paper (7th Edition) is a practical and accessible guide to understanding and evaluating clinical and scientific research papers in evidence-based healthcare. The book explains how research studies are designed, presented, and critically appraised, helping students and healthcare professionals build confidence in interpreting medical literature. Updated with modern topics such as big data, artificial intelligence, consensus methods, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and qualitative research, it balances the principles of evidence-based medicine with real-world clinical practice. Designed for medical, nursing, and allied health professionals, the book serves as an essential resource for learning how to assess research quality and apply scientific evidence effectively in healthcare decision-making.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Signal Processing: An Applied Decomposition Approach
Author: Candy
Description: Signal Processing: An Applied Decomposition Approach provides a practical and mathematically guided introduction to signal decomposition techniques used in modern signal processing. The book explains how complex signals can be broken down into individual components for analysis, noise reduction, and system modeling. It covers a wide range of decomposition methods from statistical, spectral, and model-based perspectives, including techniques such as PCA, ICA, MUSIC, SSP, and Singular Spectrum Analysis. Combining theory with real-world applications, MATLAB examples, case studies, and problem sets, the text explores both time-domain and frequency-domain signal analysis, system estimation, and modal decomposition. Designed for graduate students, researchers, and engineering professionals, the book serves as a comprehensive resource for understanding and applying advanced signal processing techniques.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Smart Antennas For 5G+
Author: Sánchez-Hernánd
Description: Smart Antennas for 5G+ explores the evolution of advanced antenna technologies designed for 5G and beyond wireless communications. The book covers key concepts such as beamforming, beam-steering, MIMO systems, mmWave antennas, metasurfaces, and reconfigurable intelligent surfaces, along with 3GPP/ITU standards, RF testing, and propagation models. It also discusses modern antenna integration techniques, flexible and 3D-printed antennas, thermal management, and performance optimization for next-generation wireless networks, making it a valuable resource for students, researchers, and engineers in 5G communications.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Nanonetworks - The Future Of Communication And Computation
Author: Lau
Description: Nanonetworks: The Future of Communication and Computation provides a clear and accessible introduction to nanoscale communication and computation systems, one of the fastest-growing interdisciplinary fields in modern technology. The book explores the foundations, applications, network paradigms, machine learning integration, and security aspects of nanonetworks, while also covering their history and key theories. With practical examples and easy-to-understand explanations, it serves as an ideal resource for students, researchers, and professionals interested in the future of communication and nanotechnology.

Subject: computer science
Title: Computational Intelligence - Theory And Applications
Author: Kumar
Description: Computational Intelligence explores advanced information processing techniques inspired by human intelligence and biological systems. The book covers key computational intelligence methods such as Artificial Neural Networks, Fuzzy Logic Systems, and Genetic Algorithms, highlighting their applications in machine learning, optimization, bioinformatics, diagnostics, and manufacturing systems. It also discusses computer vision and agile manufacturing technologies that improve flexibility, efficiency, adaptability, and productivity, making the book a valuable resource for students, researchers, and professionals in artificial intelligence and modern manufacturing.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Aircraft Design - A Systems Engineering Approach, Second Edition
Author: Sadraey
Description: Aircraft Design: A Systems Engineering Approach provides a comprehensive introduction to modern aircraft engineering by integrating aerodynamics, propulsion, flight dynamics, structures, and systems engineering into a unified design process. Covering everything from conceptual to detailed aircraft design, the book explores advanced topics such as all-electric aircraft, VTOL systems, winglets, structural and spoiler design, aircraft modernization, and current FAA requirements. With a practical and holistic approach, it serves as an essential resource for aerospace students, researchers, and engineers involved in aircraft and air vehicle design.

Subject: computer science
Title: Model Optimization Methods For Efficient And Edge Ai: Federated Learning Architectures, Frameworks And Applications
Author: Chelliah
Description: Model Optimization Methods for Efficient and Edge AI provides a comprehensive overview of AI model engineering, optimization, and deployment across cloud, edge, and hybrid environments. The book covers key AI applications such as computer vision and natural language processing, while focusing on federated learning techniques and platforms like PySyft, TensorFlow Federated, and FATE. It also explores AI model compression, edge AI for IoT systems, data privacy and security, parallel processing, and protection against cyberattacks, making it a valuable resource for students, researchers, and professionals working in AI, machine learning, and edge computing.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Analysis And Design Of Analog Integrated Circuits, 6Th Edition
Author: Gray
Description: Analysis and Design of Analog Integrated Circuits provides a comprehensive and accessible introduction to analog circuit design, covering the fundamental concepts and advanced techniques used in modern integrated circuits. The book includes detailed discussions on amplifier design, frequency response, stability, noise analysis, replica biasing, pole-zero doublets, and MOS transistor noise performance. Updated with new circuit concepts and practical design methods, it serves as an essential resource for electrical and computer engineering students, researchers, and analog IC designers.

Subject: general
Title: The Economics Of Climate Resilience In Power Infrastructure
Author: Arabnya
Description: The Economics of Climate Resilience in Power Infrastructure explains how power systems can be protected and adapted against climate-related disasters such as wildfires, hurricanes, and heat waves through engineering, economic, and resilience planning strategies. The book covers risk management, climate adaptation investments, disaster operations, post-disaster recovery, microgrid applications, and resilience-focused asset management, while using practical models and numerical examples to demonstrate how utilities and infrastructure owners can improve operational and financial stability in the face of climate challenges.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Probabilistic Forecasts And Optimal Decisions
Author: Krzysztofowicz
Description: Probabilistic Forecasts and Optimal Decisions explores how mathematical probability and decision theory can be used to improve decision-making under uncertainty. The book introduces probability concepts, continuous and discrete modeling methods, and forecasting techniques that help optimize decisions when outcomes are uncertain and influenced by multiple variables. Supported by real-world case studies, mini-projects, and exercises, it provides a practical and theoretical foundation for students, engineers, scientists, and professionals working in predictive analytics and decision analytics.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Wireless Semantic Communications - Concepts, Principles, And Challenges
Author: Sun
Description: Wireless Semantic Communications introduces the emerging field of semantic communications, where only the essential meaning of information is transmitted instead of large amounts of raw data, enabling faster and more efficient wireless communication with lower data usage and reduced ambiguity. The book explains the foundations of semantic theory, semantic encoding and decoding, transceiver design, resource management, and optimization methods, while also exploring future applications and the role of semantic communication in next-generation wireless networks. It is designed for researchers, engineers, and professionals working in wireless and communication technologies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Smart Electric And Hybrid Vehicles: Advancements In Materials, Design, Technologies, And Modeling
Author: Kumar
Description: Smart Electric and Hybrid Vehicles: Advancements in Materials, Design, Technologies, and Modeling provides a broad, interdisciplinary overview of electric and hybrid vehicle technologies, covering design, materials, modeling, and system integration from both mechanical and electrical engineering perspectives. It also discusses batteries, electric machines, power electronics, emissions reduction, regulatory and market factors, and emerging technologies like AI and digital twins, making it a comprehensive reference for engineers, researchers, and students in automotive and energy systems.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Dynamical Behaviors Of Multiweighted Complex Network Systems
Author: Wang
Description: Dynamical Behaviors of Multiweighted Complex Network Systems studies the behavior, control, and synchronization of complex dynamical and neural network systems with multiple weighted interactions, including delayed and derivative couplings. It focuses on advanced topics such as finite-time synchronization, passivity, H-infinity control, and stability under disturbances or attacks, using methods like PID and PD controllers. The book is aimed at researchers and graduate-level readers working in neural networks, complex systems, and control theory.

Subject: computer science
Title: Machine Learning And Metaheuristic Computation
Author: Cuevas
Description: Machine Learning and Metaheuristic Computation introduces the combined use of machine learning and metaheuristic optimization techniques to solve complex computational problems more efficiently. Built around Python-based implementation, it explains key concepts such as clustering, dimensionality reduction, and evolutionary algorithms like differential evolution. The book gradually guides readers from foundational ideas to advanced AI applications, making it suitable for students, researchers, and professionals interested in optimization and intelligent computation.

Subject: Engineering
Title: 3D Printing In The Healthcare Industry - Novel Applications
Author: Malviya
Description: 3D Printing in Healthcare: Medical Applications, Personalized Medicine and Surgical Innovation explores how additive manufacturing is transforming modern healthcare by enabling patient-specific solutions such as custom prosthetics, implants, surgical guides, and personalized drug delivery systems. The book covers the evolution of 3D printing, its medical applications across various diseases, and its role in surgical planning, tissue engineering, and hospital practice, while also addressing regulatory and technological challenges.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Polypropylene Cable Insulation
Author: Du
Description: Polypropylene Cable Insulation is a technical guide on the use of polypropylene as an environmentally friendly insulation material for high-voltage AC and DC power cables. It explains its dielectric properties, space charge behavior, polarization, and degradation mechanisms such as partial discharge and electrical treeing, while also presenting experimental data and applications relevant to modern power transmission systems. The book is intended for power engineers, researchers, and professionals involved in cable design, installation, and maintenance.

Subject: general
Title: Comptia Network+ Study Guide: Exam N10-009 6E
Author: Lammle
Description: CompTIA Network+ Study Guide: Exam N10-009 (6th Edition) is a certification-focused guide that prepares readers for the CompTIA Network+ N10-009 exam by covering core networking domains such as networking concepts, implementation, operations, security, and troubleshooting. Written by experienced networking professionals, it combines practical instruction with exam preparation tools like practice questions, flashcards, and glossaries, making it suitable for aspiring network administrators and support technicians aiming to build foundational networking skills and earn industry certification.

Subject: computer science

The book also includes practice problems with clear explanations to help reinforce learning. Overall, it serves as a beginner-friendly resource for students and professionals who want to build a strong foundation in probability and statistical thinking.

Subject: general
Title: Polyamory For Dummies
Author: Grant
Description: Polyamory For Dummies is a beginner-friendly guide to understanding and practicing consensual non-monogamous relationships. It explains what polyamory is, how it works in real life, and the importance of ethics, consent, and communication when managing multiple intimate relationships. The book covers key concepts like primary and secondary partners, polycules, and metamours, while also addressing common emotional challenges such as jealousy and conflict. Overall, it serves as a practical and supportive introduction for people who are curious about or exploring polyamorous relationship structures.

Subject: computer science
Title: Iapp Cipp / Us Certified Information Privacy Professional Study Guide, 2Nd Edition
Author: Chapple
Description: The IAPP CIPP/US Certified Information Privacy Professional Study Guide is a comprehensive exam-preparation book designed to help readers pass the CIPP/US certification and build expertise in U.S. data privacy law. It covers key areas such as privacy regulations, legal frameworks, government and private-sector data handling, workplace privacy, and national security considerations. The guide also includes practice exams, flashcards, and a glossary to reinforce learning. Overall, it serves both as a certification study resource and an entry point for professionals aiming to build a career in information privacy and compliance.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Connectedness: How The Best Leaders Create Authentic Human Connection In A Disconnected World
Author: Dearlove
Description: Connectedness by Des Dearlove examines how organizations can improve employee retention, productivity, and wellbeing by building authentic interpersonal relationships at work. It highlights research showing that workplace culture and the quality of human connections often matter more than compensation in influencing whether employees stay or leave. The book explores key drivers of a healthy work environment, common misconceptions about employee wellbeing, and real-world case studies that demonstrate the impact of strong workplace relationships. It is aimed at leaders, HR professionals, and managers seeking to create more engaged and productive organizations.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Coming Healthcare Revolution - 10 Forces That Will Cure America'S Health Crisis
Author: Johnson
Description: The Coming Healthcare Revolution explains the major forces transforming the U.S. healthcare system from the outside in, driven by both macro-level trends and market-level changes. It identifies ten key forces—including demographics, funding pressures, technology, and consumer-driven reforms—that are reshaping healthcare toward more accessible, efficient, and preventive care models. The book also examines issues such as rising costs, system inefficiencies, and comparisons with other countries, while highlighting shifts toward decentralized, patient-centered care. Overall, it provides a strategic overview of how healthcare is evolving and what it means for providers, policymakers, and industry stakeholders.

Subject: general
Title: The Little Book Of Hedge Funds, 2Nd Edition
Author: Scaramucci
Description: The Little Book of Hedge Funds by Anthony Scaramucci provides a clear and updated guide to understanding hedge funds in today’s changing economic environment. It explains how the industry has evolved after major financial disruptions like the 2008 crisis and COVID-19, especially in the shift from low-interest-rate conditions to higher inflation and risk. The book breaks down hedge fund strategies, manager selection, and future trends in an accessible way, helping readers understand how to navigate and make informed investment decisions in a more complex financial landscape.

Subject: computer science
Title: Cisa Certified Systems Auditor Study Guide - Covers 2024 Exam Objectives
Author: Gregory
Description: The CISA Certified Information Systems Auditor Study Guide (2024–2029) is a comprehensive exam preparation book that helps candidates master all five CISA domains, including IT governance, system auditing, information security, and business resilience. It provides clear, practical instruction aligned with real-world IS/IT audit practices, along with practice tests, flashcards, and a glossary. Designed for professionals and students, the guide not only prepares readers for the CISA exam but also strengthens their ability to assess, control, and secure enterprise information systems while understanding how IT supports business objectives.

Subject: general
Title: Making Friends As An Adult For Dummies
Author: Greene

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Minds Over Meetings: A Personal Perspective On Wellness In The Workplace
Author: Green
Description: Minds Over Meetings shares Kody Green’s personal journey living with schizophrenia while building a successful career, using it as a foundation to explore mental health and inclusion in the workplace. The book offers practical strategies for creating supportive, stigma-free environments, improving communication, and promoting employee wellbeing. It also explains workplace resources like EAPs and FMLA, while emphasizing education, empathy, and strong team relationships. Overall, it serves as a guide for both employees and leaders to better understand mental health and foster healthier, more inclusive work cultures.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Audacious Optimism: The Mindful Choice To Live Boldly And Create Endless Possibilities
Author: Adamic
Description: Audacious Optimism is a motivational memoir that follows Shanna Adamic’s journey from a successful corporate career and former cheerleader to surviving a life-threatening brain tumor and ultimately making a remarkable recovery. Through her experience, she shares how resilience, willpower, and a conscious choice to remain optimistic can help people overcome extreme adversity. The book offers insights on embracing uncertainty, cultivating inner strength, and applying optimism across all areas of life. Overall, it is an inspiring story aimed at helping readers find hope and courage during difficult challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Go-To-Market Cheat Code - The Secret To Unlocking B2B Growth
Author: Gray
Description: The Go-to-Market Cheat Code by Justin Gray and Josh Wagner explores how trust and relationships form the foundation of successful B2B growth strategies. The book explains why modern business success depends less on short-term tactics and more on building authentic, long-term trust with customers. It introduces practical frameworks like the “digital mask,” customer journey design, and the alignment of culture, customers, and context to improve business relationships and drive sustainable growth. Overall, it serves as a guide for executives and founders looking to scale their organizations by strengthening trust-driven go-to-market strategies.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Through Shifts And Shocks: Lessons From The Front Line Of Technology And Change
Author: Vamos
Description: Through Shifts and Shocks by Steve Vamos is a leadership guide focused on helping individuals and organizations navigate constant change and disruption. Drawing on his experience with major tech companies like Microsoft, Apple, IBM, and Xero, the book offers practical tools and real-world insights for aligning teams, strategy, and execution during periods of uncertainty. It emphasizes developing the right leadership mindset, prioritizing effectively, fostering psychological safety, and making tough decisions to drive innovation and growth. Overall, it serves as a practical playbook for leaders aiming to manage change and improve organizational performance.

Subject: Economics
Title: The China Business Conundrum: Ensure That "Win-Win" Doesn'T Mean Western Companies Lose Twice
Author: Wilcox
Description: The China Business Conundrum by Ken Wilcox is a firsthand account of the challenges Western companies face when attempting to operate in China. Based on the author’s experience leading a joint venture between Silicon Valley Bank and Chinese partners, the book explores cultural, regulatory, and political complexities that can complicate business collaborations. It highlights misunderstandings, operational difficulties, and hard-earned lessons from working within China’s business environment. Overall, it serves as a cautionary guide for executives and entrepreneurs seeking to navigate international partnerships in China with realistic expectations and better preparation.

Subject: computer science
Title: A Beginner'S Guide To Web Application Penetration Testing
Author: Abdollahi
Description: A Beginner’s Guide to Web Application Penetration Testing by Ali Abdollahi is an introductory cybersecurity book that teaches how to identify, test, and understand vulnerabilities in web applications. It follows OWASP standards and covers common security issues like broken access control, injection flaws, cryptographic weaknesses, and misconfigurations. The book also walks readers through the full penetration testing process—from reconnaissance and scanning to exploitation and reporting—using practical tools like Nmap and Sublist3r. Overall, it’s designed to help beginners build foundational skills in web application security and ethical hacking.

Subject: computer science
Title: Sql Essentials For Dummies
Author: Blum
Description: SQL Essentials For Dummies is a concise, quick-reference guide to the fundamentals of SQL, the standard language used to manage relational databases. It focuses on core concepts without unnecessary detail, making it useful for beginners, students, or professionals needing a refresher. The book covers essential skills for creating, managing, and manipulating databases, and serves as a practical desk reference for learning, exam preparation, or everyday work use.

Subject: computer science
Title: Java Essentials For Dummies
Author: Lowe
Description: Java Essentials For Dummies is a concise reference guide that covers the fundamental concepts of the Java programming language. It focuses on core skills needed to understand and use Java for building desktop, web, and mobile applications, without unnecessary detail or lengthy explanations. Designed as a quick refresher or learning aid, it helps readers strengthen coding basics, review key concepts, and get clear answers while working or studying.

Subject: computer science
Title: Data Science Essentials For Dummies
Author: Pierson
Description: Data Science Essentials For Dummies is a concise reference guide that covers the core concepts of data science, including data collection, cleaning, processing, and visualization. Designed for quick learning and review, it helps readers strengthen foundational skills without unnecessary detail. The book is ideal for students and professionals who want a practical refresher on key data science topics, whether for study, certification, or everyday work use.

Subject: computer science
Title: Artificial Integrity
Author: Mann
Description: Artificial Integrity by Hamilton Mann introduces the concept of building AI systems that prioritize integrity-led outcomes rather than just intelligence. It argues that traditional ethical frameworks are not enough for modern AI challenges and proposes a new “code of design” to ensure AI aligns with human values, cultural contexts, and societal norms. The book offers practical strategies for designing and implementing AI that enhances human decision-making while maintaining trust, fairness, and human agency. Overall, it serves as a guide for leaders, developers, and policymakers working toward responsible, human-centered AI systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Your Ai Roadmap: Actions To Expand Your Career, Money, And Joy
Author: Bajorek
Description: Your AI Roadmap by Dr. Joan Palmiter Bajorek is a practical career and finance guide designed to help readers navigate job uncertainty in the age of AI. It combines career strategy with personal finance and income-building advice, offering frameworks for financial literacy, goal setting, personal branding, networking, and resilience during job disruptions. Drawing on the author’s own experience in AI layoffs and building multiple income streams, the book provides actionable tools to help individuals adapt, grow, and achieve financial stability in an AI-driven economy.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Work Backwards - The Revolutionary Method To Work Smarter And Live Better
Author: Duggan
Description: Work Backwards by Tim Duggan is a practical and inspiring guide that challenges conventional thinking about work and career satisfaction. It encourages readers to rethink their approach to work-life balance by exploring global perspectives and research on happiness and productivity. The book offers actionable strategies for using flexible work models, improving wellbeing, and designing a more fulfilling career and life, helping readers move from simply coping with work to genuinely enjoying it.

Subject: computer science
Title: C++ Essentials For Dummies
Author: Mueller
Description: C++ Essentials For Dummies is a concise reference guide that covers the core concepts of the C++ programming language. It is designed for quick learning and review, helping readers understand fundamental coding concepts, refresh existing knowledge, and apply C++ in practical projects. With minimal explanations and no unnecessary detail, it serves as a handy resource for students and professionals who need a fast, reliable refresher on C++ basics for study, certification, or everyday work.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The 9% Edge Planner: A Quarterly Guide To Help You Maintain Clarity, Stay Focused, And Keep You On Track For Success
Author: Valentino
Description: The 9% Edge Yearly Planner by Candy Valentino is a practical companion to her main book that focuses on helping entrepreneurs and business leaders apply proven growth strategies in real time. It provides structured worksheets, checklists, and tools to guide users through every stage of business development—from improving revenue and customer growth to managing costs and scaling efficiently. Designed as a hands-on planning system, it helps readers turn business concepts into actionable steps for building and expanding successful enterprises.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Leading The Sustainable Business Transformation - A Playbook From Imd
Author: Binder
Description: Leading the Sustainable Business Transformation: A Playbook from IMD is a research-based guide that explains how organizations can integrate sustainability into corporate strategy while still achieving strong financial performance. Drawing on IMD research and executive case studies, it provides a practical framework for implementing environmental and social initiatives across businesses. The book addresses key challenges such as organizational barriers, performance measurement, and effective sustainability marketing, while also highlighting the role of AI and data in driving impact. Overall, it serves as a strategic playbook for leaders aiming to build sustainable, future-ready organizations.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Noise - Living And Leading When Nobody Can Focus
Author: Mccormack
Description: Noise: Living and Leading When Nobody Can Focus by Joseph McCormack explores how modern distractions—especially digital devices and constant information overload—are weakening attention spans in work and life. It offers practical techniques to help leaders and managers improve focus, reduce distractions, and prioritize effectively. Drawing on real-world examples from major organizations, the book shows how attention can be strengthened even in a hyper-connected world and how doing so improves leadership, communication, and decision-making.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Unapologetically Adhd: A Step-By-Step Framework For Everyday Planning On Your Terms
Author: Kinzer
Description: Unapologetically ADHD by Nikki Kinzer and Pete D. Wright is a practical and supportive guide designed to help people with ADHD manage planning, organization, and long-term goals in a flexible, realistic way. It explains how the ADHD brain works, including challenges like all-or-nothing thinking and rejection sensitivity, while offering coaching strategies, exercises, and tools to improve daily functioning. The book also focuses on reducing shame and building self-acceptance, helping readers create structure that works with their strengths rather than against them.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: How To Breathe While Suffocating: A Story Of Overcoming Addiction, Recovering From Trauma, And Healing My Soul
Author: Brackett
Description: How To Breathe While Suffocating by Bruce W. Brackett is an inspirational and reflective book that encourages readers to overcome feelings of hopelessness, self-doubt, and emotional struggle. Drawing from the author’s personal experiences with trauma and recovery, it combines affirmations, storytelling, and insight to help readers reconnect with purpose and resilience. The book addresses themes like loneliness, mental health, and post-crisis disconnection, ultimately guiding readers toward self-acceptance, positivity, and the motivation to keep moving forward in life.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Capitalism Created The Climate Crisis And Capitalism Will Solve It: The Market Forces Catalyzing A Climate Technology Renaissance
Author: Kawamori
Description: Capitalism Created the Climate Crisis and Capitalism Will Solve It by Kentaro Kawamori examines the relationship between free-market systems and global warming, arguing that capitalism both contributed to the climate crisis and has the potential to drive its solution. The book explores how governments, finance, and fossil fuel industries have shaped environmental outcomes, while highlighting how climate technology, innovation, and market incentives can help reverse damage. It also discusses the future role of energy systems and climate-focused stakeholders in accelerating change, positioning capitalism as a potential driver of large-scale climate solutions.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Power Of Going All-In - Secrets For Success In Business, Leadership, And Life
Author: Bornancin
Description: The Power of Going All-In by Brandon Bornancin is a leadership and business guide focused on building high-performing teams and achieving large-scale success. Drawing on the author’s entrepreneurial experience, it offers practical strategies for motivating employees, defining vision and purpose, eliminating poor leadership habits, and improving team performance through coaching and data-driven insights. The book serves as a hands-on toolkit for entrepreneurs and managers who want to strengthen leadership skills, inspire teams, and drive stronger business results.

Subject: general
Title: The Warren Buffett Way, 30Th Anniversary Edition
Author: Hagstrom
Description: The Warren Buffett Way (30th Anniversary Edition) by Robert Hagstrom is a detailed exploration of Warren Buffett’s investment philosophy and long-term wealth-building approach. It breaks down Buffett’s core principles of business-driven investing, including how to evaluate companies, build a strong portfolio, and think with a long-term mindset. The book also explores investor psychology, common mistakes to avoid, and insights from Charlie Munger’s “worldly wisdom,” along with historical Berkshire Hathaway portfolio data. Overall, it serves as a practical guide for investors who want to apply Buffett’s proven strategies to their own investing decisions.

Subject: general
Title: On A Discourse That Might Not Be A Semblance - The Seminar Of Jacques Lacan, Book Xviii
Author: Lacan
Description: This passage describes Jacques Lacan’s psychoanalytic theory of sexuality as formalized by Jacques-Alain Miller. It argues that human sexual relations are not purely biological but structured through “semblance” (social and symbolic displays) and an underlying real dimension of jouissance that differs between men and women. Lacan suggests that masculine sexuality is organized around the phallic function and symbolic display, while feminine jouissance is less fully contained within these structures, making it harder to systematize. The text ultimately points toward a formal, logical approach to sexuality—using symbolic and mathematical notation—to explain Lacan’s controversial idea that “there is no sexual relationship” in the sense of a fully harmonious, universal structure between sexes.

Subject: general
Title: First Writings
Author: Lacan
Description: The passage explains that Jacques Lacan’s early work as a psychiatrist already shows signs of his later psychoanalytic theory. His approach focused on the uniqueness of each patient case (“singularity”) and careful attention to detailed clinical observations, even when complex or difficult to follow. It also highlights early traces of his later ideas, such as using “structure” instead of developmental explanations, analyzing patients’ writings, and linking psychological symptoms with literature and creative expression.

Subject: general
Title: Distant Freedom: Essay On Chekhov
Author: Rancière
Description: Chekhov’s stories, as interpreted by Jacques Rancière, suggest that life is not fixed or repetitive—“another life is possible” always exists within ordinary moments. His fiction captures brief openings where change, freedom, or a different way of living can emerge against everyday monotony. For Rancière, literature doesn’t just convey knowledge; it reshapes how we see reality by revealing these possibilities and encouraging us to imagine and strive toward more liberated forms of existence.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Biobanks In Healthcare - From The Collection Of Biological Samples To Digital Health
Author: Arrighi
Description: Biobanks are now crucial institutions in modern healthcare, responsible for managing and governing valuable biological samples needed for research and medical innovation. With advances in biotechnology, precision medicine, and the rise of large-scale digital health data, organized access to human biological materials has become essential for identifying biomarkers, developing biological drugs, and ensuring reproducible scientific results. Biobanks in Healthcare highlights how these systems are transforming medical research by improving data quality and supporting the growth of precision medicine in the digital era.

Subject: general
Title: Thermal Properties Measurement Of Materials, 2Nd Edition
Author: Jannot
Description: Energy efficiency in industry and buildings is a major driver of the global energy transition, making it essential to use advanced insulating materials and high-performance components. This requires precise understanding and measurement of thermal properties to select the right materials and apply accurate testing methods. Thermal Properties Measurement of Materials (2nd Edition) serves as a practical guide to help users choose and implement appropriate measurement techniques for characterizing materials, making it useful for both thermal engineers and non-specialists involved in energy and materials science.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Biomedical Devices And Sensors
Author: Molimard
Description: Medical device design is a highly regulated field that sits at the intersection of engineering, healthcare, and ethics. While modern digital health tools like smartphones and wearables make body monitoring seem simple, true medical devices require strict compliance with safety, quality, and legal standards to protect human life. This book introduces biomedical students to the field by covering its historical and economic background, key design principles, and essential EU and US regulations. It also explains sensor technologies used in human health monitoring and highlights the importance of clinical evaluation and risk management. Overall, it provides a structured overview of how medical devices are responsibly designed, tested, and validated in real-world healthcare systems.

Subject: general
Title: Geopolitics And Energy Transition 2 - From One Continent To Another, Contrasting Situations
Author: Favennec
Description: The book Geopolitics and Energy Transition 2 explores how the global energy landscape is changing as countries move from fossil fuel dependence toward cleaner energy systems. It highlights the central challenge of reducing CO₂ emissions while still relying on fossil fuels, since renewable energy remains intermittent and limited in share. The book also compares regional energy situations across continents—such as North America’s resource wealth, Europe’s import dependence, Africa’s access challenges, and Asia’s rising demand—showing how geopolitics, resources, and energy transition are deeply interconnected worldwide.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Some Complex Phenomena In Fluid And Solid Mechanics
Author: Saouab
Description: Some Complex Phenomena in Fluid and Solid Mechanics brings together research themes explored by Abdelhak Ambari, focusing on areas such as rheology of complex fluids, hydrodynamic interactions, low Reynolds number flows, porous media characterization, instabilities, solid mechanics, and electrochemical metrology. The book is aimed at researchers and advanced students, offering both theoretical and experimental insights, while also serving as an accessible introduction to key methods and approaches in fluid and solid mechanics.

Subject: computer science
Title: Digital Presences Of Organizations
Author: Pinède
Description: Digital Presences of Organizations examines how organizations communicate in today’s digital ecosystem, where websites, social networks, and data-driven platforms shape how information is delivered and perceived. It highlights key challenges in creating effective online communication strategies, especially in multicultural contexts. The book explores three main areas: differentiated content and interaction strategies based on organization type and users, the cultural dimensions of local versus global communication, and the role of storytelling in building online narratives.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Design And Evaluation Of Ad Hoc Routing Protocol
Author: Wahl
Description: Design and Evaluation of Ad Hoc Routing Protocol focuses on vehicle-based ad hoc communication systems in environments without fixed network infrastructure. It explores the challenges of supporting driver assistance services under such conditions and presents a routing protocol designed by the authors as a case study. The book explains its design methodology, functional requirements, and performance evaluation, helping readers understand how such protocols work and are assessed. It is intended for beginners in protocol engineering and intelligent transport systems, while also offering useful insights and methods for more advanced researchers.

Subject: general
Title: Bioreactor Implementation In The Agro-Food Industries - Technology, Kinetics And Modelization
Author: Ghoul
Description: Bioreactor Implementation in the Agro-Food Industries explains the growing use of bioreactors in producing food ingredients under controlled, efficient, and environmentally friendly conditions. It covers key engineering principles such as thermodynamics, material and heat balances, microbial kinetics, and process optimization needed to design and run bioreactors effectively. The book is structured into theory and applications, first introducing core bioreactor concepts and then providing practical examples of producing valuable food ingredients and metabolites, making it useful for students, researchers, and industry professionals.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Emotional Intelligence 2E
Author: Hasson
Description: Emotional Intelligence (2nd Edition) by Gill Hasson explains that success and well-being depend more on how well we understand and manage emotions than on intelligence or talent alone. The book provides practical guidance on developing emotional intelligence (EQ) to improve happiness, confidence, and performance in both personal and professional life. It teaches readers how to manage stress and anxiety, communicate clearly, become more assertive, and handle workplace challenges like conflict, politics, and bullying more effectively.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Stahlschlüssel - Key to Steel, 2025 Edition
Author: V.S. Wegst
Description: Stahlschlüssel - Key to Steel, 2025 Edition is a comprehensive steel cross-reference guide that helps users identify, compare, and find equivalent steel grades and material designations worldwide. Covering over 75,000 standards and steel grades from hundreds of manufacturers, this trilingual reference (English, French, and German) is an essential resource for engineers, designers, materials specialists, and technical professionals.

Subject: Food
Title: Food Production Operations - Fourth Edition
Author: Dr Parvinder S. Bali
Description: Food Production Operations is a practical guide for aspiring chefs and hospitality professionals, covering essential kitchen operations, cooking techniques, food commodities, pastry basics, and Indian cuisine. Enriched with real-world insights and chef’s tips drawn from sixteen years of experience with Oberoi Hotels and Resorts, the book bridges the gap between culinary theory and professional kitchen practice.

Subject: Hotel Management
Title: Hotel Housekeeping
Author: G Raghubalan & Smritee Raghubalan
Description: Hotel Housekeeping: Operations and Management is a comprehensive guide to housekeeping operations and management in the hospitality industry. Fully aligned with the NCHMCT Accommodation Operations syllabus, it serves as an essential resource for students, educators, and hotel professionals, earning its reputation as the “Housekeeper’s Bible.”

Subject: Hotel Management
Title: Hotel Front Office - Third Edition
Author: Jatashankar R. Tewari
Description: Hotel Front Office is a comprehensive guide to front office operations and management, designed for hotel management students and hospitality professionals. Featuring updated industry practices, real-world examples, case studies, and project assignments, the book offers both theoretical knowledge and practical insights into the functioning of a hotel's front office department.

Subject: Food
Title: Food and Beverage Service - Third Edition
Author: R. Singaravelavan
Description: Food and Beverage Service is a comprehensive guide to food service operations, menu planning, beverages, bar management, and ancillary functions. Updated to reflect current industry practices, the book combines theoretical knowledge with practical insights, making it an essential resource for hotel management students and hospitality professionals alike.
